question: Will Netanyahu cease to be PM of Israel by Dec 31, 2026?
narrative_context: Netanyahu re-elected as Likud leader, targets Sept/Oct 2026 elections — preference for late-year vote makes Dec 31 exit extremely unlikely (Anadolu Agency, March 2026)
Corruption trial verdict not expected until 2027 — no conviction mechanism removing him in calendar 2026 (Wikipedia: Trial of Benjamin Netanyahu)
Israeli coalition formation takes 6-12 weeks minimum historically (Chatham House, March 2026) — Oct elections put new PM in late Dec or Jan 2027 at best
Two budget paths: (1) Pass budget (~55%) → Netanyahu controls timing → Oct elections → near-zero Dec 31 exit; (2) Budget fails (~45%) → June elections → narrow sub-50% exit path
Only 2 of last 5 Israeli PMs left office within 9 months of active war/major escalation — base rate strongly against short-timeline exits
thesis: Market prices Netanyahu at 49.5% to leave by Dec 31, but electoral timeline makes this unlikely. If budget passes (55% likely), elections move to Oct, requiring 6-12 weeks of coalition formation — new PM arrives in late Dec/Jan, missing deadline. If budget fails (45%), June elections still leave only 6 months for coalition, and Netanyahu likely stays or leads new coalition (Gantz recently signaled willingness). Corruption trial verdict in 2027, not 2026. Historical base rate: only 2 of 5 recent Israeli PMs exited within 9 months. Real probability: 28%.
x_thread: Market says 49.5% Netanyahu is out by Dec 31. But if budget passes (55% likely), elections move to Oct → coalition takes 6-12 weeks → new PM in Jan, not Dec. 🧵
If budget fails (45%), June elections still need 6+ months of coalition formation. Netanyahu leads negotiation or stays. Base rate: only 2/5 recent PMs exit in 9 months.
Corruption trial verdict: 2027, not 2026. Only removal mechanism is election timing, and that timeline doesn't work for Dec 31 exit.
Netanyahu controls election timing. He has incentive to delay into 2027. Real odds: ~28%, not 49.5%. https://dub.sh/pb-x
